description: Global intercept in the log-linear predictor before identified-space re-centering.
Regression coefficients for covariates x1 (continuous) and x2 (binary, time-constant within region in the final DGP).
ICAR spatial random effect at the coarsest level; represented in the identified space with a sum-to-zero constraint.
ICAR precision; larger values imply stronger spatial smoothing.
Negative-binomial size parameter; smaller values imply more overdispersion, and r → ∞ approaches Poisson.
Negative-binomial multiplicative random effect; κ ~ Gamma(r, r), so E(κ)=1 conditionally on r.
Residual risk process evolving through the Beta-discounted Gamma state equation.
Initial residual risk state at time 0.
Coarse-level discount factor; smaller values imply faster adaptation and shorter memory. In the current DGP, scalar scenario values are expanded to a length-n1 vector.
Split proportions from each coarse node to its n_children children; rows sum to 1.
Dirichlet discount factor; smaller values imply faster adaptation of split proportions.
Effective offset excluding lambda_tilde and kappa: xi = e · exp(beta0 + x'β + φ).
Fine-level probabilities or allocation shares implied by omega.
Full coarsest-level Poisson mean: mu = xi · lambda_tilde · kappa.
Known exposure or population offset at the coarsest level.
Continuous region-specific covariate generated as a smooth AR(1)-type process over time.
Binary region-specific covariate that is constant over time within each region in the final DGP.
Observed count at the coarsest level (l = 1).
Observed child-level counts at level 2; y_fine[t,j,] sums to y_coarse[t,j].
Initial Gamma filter shape for lambda_tilde.
Initial Gamma filter rate for lambda_tilde.
Initial Dirichlet concentration for omega.
Identified-space truth for the intercept after absorbing the global re-centering shift; used for fair posterior comparison.
Identified-space spatial truth after absorbing region-specific re-centering shifts; used for fair posterior comparison.
Identified-space latent residual-risk path used for posterior recovery checks.
Region-specific shift used to map raw log residual risk paths into the identified parameterization; not an inferential target.
Global shift absorbed into the identified intercept; not an inferential target.
